objections.objection_text: Working hard or taking work seriously will pull me away from God.
objections.underlying_belief: God and the material world are mutually exclusive; I can't serve two masters.
objections.underlying_fear: Loss of spiritual peace; becoming a 'factory worker' or 'obedient employee' instead of a free man.
objections.counter_truth_angle: Work is the place where faith is tested and practiced; it is a vehicle for devotion, not an impediment.
objections.transcript_proof_quote_redacted: devoting myself to work makes me fearful of just being like this like too obedient little good employee factory worker. And I'm like, that's not what God wants.

objections.objection_text: I can't start my spiritual journey because my life is too messy (I'm in 'sin').
objections.underlying_belief: Salvation required me to be perfect first.
objections.underlying_fear: Rejection from God; being 'too far gone'.
objections.counter_truth_angle: Jesus died precisely because we are imperfect; salvation is about being reborn, not being perfect.
objections.transcript_proof_quote_redacted: he knows we're imperfect, but he really just wants us to have pure hearts... dying for our sins means... a way for us to liberate our own hearts.
